crossRefs.label: Remove the passenger side front seat.

blocks.text: Turn ignition OFF (LOCK) mode. Remove the passenger side front seat.

refs.label: Remove the passenger side front seat.

blocks.text: Disconnect the suction tube (A) from the fuel pump and install the hand vacuum pump (B).

items.text: If negative pressure is present, proceed to the next step "Suction Line Inspection 1".
If negative pressure is present, proceed to the next step "Suction Line Inspection 1".
If there is no negative pressure, Replace the fuel filter.
If there is no negative pressure, Replace the fuel filter.

blocks.text: Turn ignition OFF (LOCK) mode. Attach the hand vacuum pump (A) to the suction tube (B) and apply negative pressure. Check to see if negative pressure is maintained.

items.text: If negative pressure is retained, inspect the following areas
If negative pressure is retained, inspect the following areas
Clogged suction line
Clogged suction line
Clogged suction line
Suction joint (3-way joint)
Suction joint (3-way joint)
If negative pressure does not hold, proceed to the next step, "Lift Up".
If negative pressure does not hold, proceed to the next step, "Lift Up".

blocks.text: Disconnect the breather tube (B) on the filler neck side from the suction joint (3-way joint) (A), and attach a rubber plug (C) or similar device to the suction joint (3-way joint).
Apply negative pressure to the suction tube (B) with the hand vacuum pump (A).

items.text: If negative pressure persists, check for a blocked breather line between the suction joint (3-way joint) and the fuel tank breather valve. Replace the fuel tank.
If negative pressure persists, check for a blocked breather line between the suction joint (3-way joint) and the fuel tank breather valve. Replace the fuel tank.
If negative pressure does not hold, proceed to the next step, Breather Line Inspection 2.
If negative pressure does not hold, proceed to the next step, Breather Line Inspection 2.

blocks.text: Lift up the vehicle. Attach a hand vacuum pump (B) to the breather tube (A) on the filler neck side to provide negative pressure. Check to see if negative pressure is maintained.

items.text: If negative pressure persists, check for a clogged breather line from the suction joint (3-way joint) to the filler neck.
If negative pressure persists, check for a clogged breather line from the suction joint (3-way joint) to the filler neck.
If negative pressure does not hold, proceed to the next step "Suction line inspection 2".
If negative pressure does not hold, proceed to the next step "Suction line inspection 2".

blocks.text: Remove the rubber plug (B), etc. from the suction joint (3-way joint) (A) and reconnect the breather tube (C).
Attach the pressurized side of the hand vacuum pump (A) to the suction tube (B) and check that it pressurizes and holds pressure.

items.text: Breather line and suction line normal if pressure holds.
Breather line and suction line normal if pressure holds.
If pressure does not hold, inspect the following areas
If pressure does not hold, inspect the following areas
Torn suction tube
Torn suction tube
Torn suction tube
Suction joint (3-way joint)
Suction joint (3-way joint)
